bullets: A 372,590-shipment freight panel from a retailer that decides which shipments to intervene on to avoid costly cancellations; period and geography not stated.
Prompted GPT-4o and Claude Sonnet 4.6 rank cancellation probability against calibrated tabular models, logistic regression, and a fallback rule, checked by AUC and out-of-time validation.

GPT-4o ranks competitively at AUC 0.810 versus 0.808 for XGBoost and 0.832 for logistic regression, but its probabilities exceed the cost threshold, so it intervenes on every shipment and saves nothing.
validation_note: AUC against realized cancellations, out-of-time test

bullets: 4,416 US small and micro-cap equities over 2021 to 2026, sharing a point-in-time panel of prices, 46.8M XBRL facts, 53 macro series, 295,860 SEC filings, and 215,882 news articles.
The benchmark scores 19 methods across six families, from naive heuristics to time-series foundation models, fine-tuned LLM time-series models, and zero-shot LLMs; specific model names not stated.
Introduces seven tasks spanning contextual forecasting, public and private valuation, statement generation, and scenario-conditioned returns; no aggregate accuracy figure is reported in the abstract.
China's Local Government Financing Vehicles used as an empirical case of institutionalized information asymmetry, implicit state credit, and hidden transfer of financial burdens to the public.
Several unnamed LLM architectures interpret the dual-track LGFV mechanism, with ChatGPT screened out for sycophancy and semantic evasion; comparison is qualitative with no accuracy benchmark.

Institutional-weighted models over-index sanitized official data and show systemic blindness, while social-weighted models detect more risk but remain vulnerable to coordinated astroturfing narratives.
Tourism futures for the Middle East and North Africa over the next decade, using synthetic AI expert judgment across three iterative Delphi rounds; unit is AI-generated scenario input.
ChatGPT, Gemini, DeepSeek, Copilot, and Claude serve as synthetic expert informants to map convergence, divergence, and uncertainty; no validation against human experts is reported.
Identifies climate change and extreme heat, geopolitical instability, and water scarcity as the most consequential existential risks for MENA tourism over the coming decade.
Real open-ended exam responses from a single university course and one instructor, graded across two independent rounds conducted at different times.
LLMs, family not stated, are fine-tuned on the instructor's historical grades; AI grades are compared with the two human rounds, with no accuracy check against a ground truth.

AI-human grading discrepancies are significantly associated with intra-rater grading variability, suggesting the model flags responses whose grades are unstable while leaving final decisions to the instructor.
Straight-through underwriting of small commercial Business Owner Policies in a synthetic but realistic experimental environment reflecting regulated actuarial decision workflows.

Three pipelines are compared, a single-LLM baseline, a naive RAG system, and a multi-agent agentic RAG with retrieval, third-party checks, and multi-step rules; models not named, no accuracy figure given.
The agentic RAG pipeline performs best overall, with the largest gains in multi-step and missing-information cases where retrieval and reflection avoid unsupported straight-through decisions.
Two experiments on entity citability in generative AI engines: 108 responses in the accommodation sector of Foz do Iguacu, and 5,544 responses across 82 hospitality and ERP entities.
Gemini 2.5 Flash, Claude Sonnet 4.6, and GPT-4o were queried via official APIs in May 2026 with branded, categorical, and problem formulations; citation rates were recorded directly.

Citability depends on the engine-by-vertical pair; branded queries cite the entity 96.8 to 100 percent of the time, while discovery queries vary sharply, for example Gemini's 0.368 delta in hospitality versus 0.828 in ERP.
